title: Bidens macroptera
intro: Bidens macroptera, also known as giant beggar ticks or giant tickseed, is a flowering plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Bidens macroptera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Bidens macroptera is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and topical preparations, such as creams and salves, which are traditionally used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and inflammation.
taxonomy: Bidens macroptera is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the tribe Madinae and is part of the genus Bidens. This plant is native to tropical regions and is characterized by its distinctive leaves and flowers. The classification of Bidens macroptera is further supported by its similarity to other species within the Bidens genus. Overall, the classification of Bidens macroptera reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Asteraceae family.
